bugs_found.description: Order.total() subtracts discount as a flat amount instead of treating it as a percentage. Setting discount=10 subtracts $10 instead of 10%.
bugs_found.fix: Changed to multiply subtotal by (1 - discount_percent/100) for percentage-based discount.

bugs_found.description: check_stock uses > instead of >= for comparison, rejecting orders when requested quantity exactly equals stock.
bugs_found.fix: Changed > to >= so that stock of 5 allows ordering 5 items.

bugs_found.description: Field named 'discount' is ambiguous - used as both a flat amount in total() and set as a percentage from processor.py.
bugs_found.fix: Renamed to discount_percent for clarity and updated total() to use percentage math.

interaction_analysis: The bugs interact across files: processor.py passes a percentage value (discount_percent=10) to order.discount, but models.py Order.total() subtracts it as a flat dollar amount. This cross-file mismatch means the discount calculation is fundamentally wrong. Additionally, inventory.py's off-by-one error in check_stock (> instead of >=) causes reserve_stock to fail when exact stock is requested, even though processor.py correctly calls reserve_stock.
